key_insights.insight: Understanding what you enjoy and what you're intrinsically good at is crucial for fulfillment in your career.
key_insights.quote: I think it's about understanding what you enjoy. I think it starts there. And then whether you're playing what you're intrinsically good at in those areas as well.

key_insights.insight: Gathering feedback from trusted colleagues and mentors can help you identify your strengths and weaknesses, leading to personal and professional growth.
key_insights.quote: Often you're not the best judge of yourself, and so finding people around you, either family and friends, but actually also work colleagues that can start to say, what is it about me that's good? What is it about me that maybe isn't so good?

key_insights.insight: Empathy and understanding the motivations of your team members are essential for effective leadership.
key_insights.quote: I lead with empathy and that it really matters to me to understand who the people are that are, that I'm working with and what motivates them and, and what doesn't.

key_insights.insight: Having clear deliverables and expectations, along with regular one-to-one meetings with your team, fosters constant updates and provides opportunities for guidance.
key_insights.quote: I think I set pretty clear deliverables and expectations and I really spend the time with my core team. I absolutely believe in the frequency of a regular one-to-one meeting where we can learn, you know, to get those constant updates where I can give advice.

key_insights.insight: Being willing to pivot and change your mind based on data is crucial for success.
key_insights.quote: I'm not afraid to pivot and to change my mind. If I see evidence and data, there's a poster at wall, data wins arguments.

key_insights.insight: Creating a culture where employees can bring their whole selves to work leads to higher performance and greater happiness.
key_insights.quote: Allowing the space and the culture within companies to be who you are and to know the things that matter to you ultimately allows people to be higher performers, which ultimately means that they'll do better in their jobs and they'll be happier in their lives.

key_insights.insight: Leaders must lead by example, demonstrating vulnerability and openness to encourage others to do the same.
key_insights.quote: Showing that vulnerability, being so open, bringing your whole self allows other people to do so as well.

key_insights.insight: Negotiating for what you want requires doing your homework and understanding your own worth in the market.
key_insights.quote: Not just thank you, but I've been out and I've looked at the market and I understand now that my value is this.

key_insights.insight: Nervousness can be a sign of growth and a rush of adrenaline before achieving something new.
key_insights.quote: The times I've grown the most have been the times where I felt most nervous, the times that I thought I wasn't going to be able to do it.

actionable_advice.advice: Create an informal board of advisors of people a few years ahead of you in their career.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify individuals with relevant experience, seek their advice on specific challenges or opportunities, and adapt their insights to your own situation.

actionable_advice.advice: Schedule regular one-on-one meetings with team members.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dedicate time for individual check-ins to provide guidance, gather updates, and foster open communication.

actionable_advice.advice: If you are not being offered a clear path to where you want to be, look at other opportunities.
actionable_advice.how_to: If your request has been rejected, evaluate if you see yourself staying long term and consider that the rejection could be a wake-up call to find something that better suits your needs.

actionable_advice.advice: Before salary negotiations, research market rates for your role and experience.
actionable_advice.how_to: Utilize online resources, network with industry professionals, and gather data to support your desired salary range.

memorable_stories: The speaker shares a story about women leaving their coats and handbags on chairs to pretend they were still at work when they were leaving to fulfill other responsibilities.
The speaker talks about the first time she had a pay review and how she would simply accept what was offered to her, not knowing her own worth.
contrarian_claims: Bringing your whole self to work, including personal issues, can lead to better performance, challenging the traditional view of maintaining a strictly professional persona at work.
Feelings of nervousness before a big moment should be viewed as excitement and adrenaline, rather than imposter syndrome.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 84 - VP Of META Explains How To Get Exactly What You Want: Nicola Mendelsohn
